answer:
Honestly, I think one of the most valuable things I learned this year is how to deal with failure in a healthier way. I used to be really hard on myself whenever something didn’t go as planned, especially with university applications and, you know, all the big decisions about my future. But my older sister actually gave me some really good advice. She told me that failure isn’t always a setback—it can be an opportunity to grow or discover a new direction. At first, I was kind of skeptical, but after missing out on my dream internship, I realized she was right. Instead of just feeling defeated, I decided to reflect on what went wrong and what I could improve. It really helped me develop resilience and, honestly, a more positive mindset.

This change has made a noticeable difference in my life. I find that I’m more open to taking risks, like applying for programs that seem competitive or trying new activities. Also, it’s made me more empathetic toward others who are struggling, because I know how tough things can get. As for sharing this wisdom, I try to lead by example with my friends. Whenever someone in my group feels like giving up, I remind them that setbacks aren’t the end of the world—and sometimes, they’re the start of something better. I mean, just being honest about my own experiences helps others feel less alone. So, yeah, that lesson has definitely made me wiser, and I hope I can keep passing it on.
title: Name something you learned this year that has made you wiser. Who taught you this, and how has it improved your life? How will you share your new wisdom with others?
